commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=86ebda8b8431fd6c0a9ff24ff977e1114fd8f2ab)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds optional `rawBody` to `before_prompt_build`, `before_agent_start`, and `agent_end` hook events and threads clean user input through reply, queue, steering, embedded-runner, docs, changelog, and SDK API hash paths.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding by source inspection: PR-head docs say steered messages leave `rawBody` undefined, while PR-head code passes `rawBody` through active-run steering and emits `currentRawBody` on `agent_end`. I did not run a live channel/plugin reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs contributor real behavior proof plus maintainer/API judgment on steered-message semantics; automation should not repair while the external proof gate is unmet.

target.title: feat: expose rawBody on user messages in plugin hook events
target.body:
### Summary

Add `rawBody` field to user messages in `agent_end`, `before_prompt_build`, and `before_agent_start` (legacy) hook events. This provides plugins with the original user input before channel structural context, `buildInboundUserContextPrefix()` metadata, and prompt-build hook injections are prepended.

### Motivation

Plugin hooks receive `event.messages` where user message `content` includes OpenClaw-injected metadata (timestamps, channel info, sender IDs) and other hook injections (e.g., memory context). Plugins that process user content for purposes like memory extraction, summarization, or analytics must parse and strip this metadata â€” a fragile approach since the format varies by channel, chat type, and OpenClaw version.

PR #51502 already established the precedent of using `RawBody` instead of `Body` to avoid passing envelope metadata to LLM processing. This PR extends that principle to plugin hook events.

### Changes

- Compute `rawBodyForHooks` from `CommandBody ?? RawBody ?? Body` in the channel message handler â€” the clean user text without @mentions, sender labels, or message annotations
- Thread `rawBody` through the run params chain: `get-reply-run.ts` â†’ `runReplyAgent` â†’ `runAgentTurnWithFallback` â†’ `runEmbeddedPiAgent` â†’ `runEmbeddedAttempt`
- Include `rawBody` in hook event objects for `before_prompt_build`, `before_agent_start`, and `agent_end`
- Add `rawBody` to `FollowupRun` type so the value survives queueing/deferred runs
- `rawBody` is `undefined` for non-channel runs (CLI, heartbeat, cron)

### Backward Compatibility

- Additive optional field only â€” no breaking changes
- Plugins can feature-detect `rawBody` and fall back to `content`:
  ```typescript
  const userText = event.rawBody ?? extractText(lastUser.content);
  ```
- Plugin SDK API baseline unchanged (no drift)

### Use Case

Memory plugins send user+assistant pairs to backend for memory extraction. Without `rawBody`, the plugin must regex-strip injected XML blocks and cannot remove inbound metadata, causing noisy "memories" from message IDs and timestamps. With `rawBody`, the plugin gets clean user input directly.
target.author: kingdoooo

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
